what_it_does: An append-only, per-member finds log for fork ch-8djrpmsl that records verification states, built-code locations, falsifiable tests, live end-to-end verification notes, open gaps, and handoff contracts across the graph-of-minds substrate, address resolver/territory layer, operator surface/RHM, model routing, vi-vision, decision registry, channels boundary, spotlight, and tool-face invoke layer.
observations: Uses a per-find format: what · where(path:line) · verbatim-if-quotable · verification-state · what-shape-it-points-at.
Labels verification states explicitly: VERIFIED-BY-USE, BUILT-UNVERIFIED, BUILT + VERIFIED, INFERENCE, DESIGN-ONLY, ZERO, OPEN, HELD, PENDING.
The graph-of-minds substrate is described as built and addressable but missing a renderer.
Records many live end-to-end verifications: channels boundary A–I, spotlight pointing, decision grounding, V brain, route-back write.
Contains operational incident notes: decision_take test-contamination, retract primitive, verify-mode guards, attribution-token design.
Includes explicit 'STOOD DOWN', 'HOLD', 'NEXT', and handoff boundaries to other members/channels.
Tracks bridge-restart dependencies for import-time code changes vs request-time fresh record loading.
standouts: The graph-of-minds substrate is built and addressable today; the renderer is the consistently missing half.
territory_for resolves any address scheme through the ONE spine and renders human-only operator context without leaking addresses.
Decision registry + retract primitive + test-contamination cleanup: a trust-critical operational incident and fix.
Channels boundary passed full LIVE-LOCAL-VERIFY A–I, making Claude Design a two-way channel peer on Supabase.
Spotlight 'point at the thing' built end-to-end: brain emits point(token), client maps token→ui://, surface spotlights in sync with speech.
Tool-face invoke layer (gaps 1-3) completed with a unified operator-attribution floor design.
conventions: Append-only finds file owned by one member; lead synthesizes across all finds into DISCOVERY_LOG + EMERGING_SHAPE.
Honest verification-state labels; findings are not conclusions.
Heavy use of file:line/path citations and commit hashes.
Operator-law: no raw addresses/schemes in human-facing prose.
Fail-loud behavior and degrade-clean notes preferred.
Reuse-don't-parallel: reuse existing resolver/territory legs rather than building parallel paths.
Security posture: least-privilege principals, read-only vs write-gated, denylist env scrub, attribution tokens for consequential writes.
concerns: The file is extremely long and mixes many work-streams, making it hard to scan for a single topic.
Many 'live' statuses depend on a bridge restart that may not yet have happened; the file notes this but it is easy to miss.
Test-contamination incidents show that verification drives can write real operator-attributed marks.
Numerous items are HELD or OPEN awaiting other members, so the file contains a lot of unresolved dependencies.
Verification-state taxonomy is applied informally; no strict schema enforces the labels.
notes: A dense operational diary of fork ch-8djrpmsl's substrate and interface work, emphasizing verified-by-use status, operator-law, and explicit handoff/gate boundaries.
questions: Which bridge restart commit range is currently live in production?
Has the #1b per-session operator-token attribution floor been implemented and activated beyond the design slot?
What is the exact current status of the dna/voice.operator_voice.rhm_spoken content calibration?
Have all HELD items (e.g., vi-vision write governance, #71 Phase 2 wiring, subtype-briefing model) been resolved or still pending?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a per-member append-only finds log recording honest verification states and shape pointers, exactly as stated in the header.
apparent_themes: Verification-by-use as the primary epistemic standard.
Addressable substrate: everything is a node with a URI-like address and projections.
Operator-law: human-facing surfaces must never leak internal identifiers.
Least-privilege and attribution for consequential mutations.
intention_signals: To create a transparent, falsifiable record of what code is built, what is verified, what remains open, and what shape the emerging interface should take, so the lead can synthesize convergences without losing the honest uncertainty of each member's lane.
novelty: An unusually granular verification-state ledger that combines commit-level build notes, live end-to-end gate results, security incident cleanup, and explicit 'do not build yet' holds in one per-member file.
contribution: A durable, append-only verification ledger that preserves honest uncertainty, test contamination lessons, live gate statuses, and handoff contracts so a fused system can trace which claims are proven, which are inferred, and which await another member's input.
summary_for_embedding: Fork ch-8djrpmsl's per-member finds log documenting verification states and live end-to-end results for the graph-of-minds substrate, address resolver, operator surface, channels boundary, spotlight, and tool-face invoke layer.
intention_for_embedding: To record honest, falsifiable build and verification notes for synthesis by the lead into the shared DISCOVERY_LOG and EMERGING_SHAPE.
